questions.text: Hammerling’s experiments of Acetabularia involved exchanging:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Factual Recall. Acetabularia used in Hammerling’s nucleocytoplasmic experiment is unicellular uninucleate green algae. Hammerling’s experiment on Acetabularia involved exchanging rhizoid and stalk. Presence of hereditary information in the nucleous was proved by the work of Hammerling on single celled alga Acetabularia.

Factual Recall. Acetabularia used in Hammerling’s nucleocytoplasmic experiment is unicellular uninucleate green algae. Hammerling’s experiment on Acetabularia involved exchanging rhizoid and stalk. Presence of hereditary information in the nucleous was proved by the work of Hammerling on single celled alga Acetabularia.

Presence of hereditary information in the nucleous was proved by the work of Hammerling on single celled alga Acetabularia.

Presence of hereditary information in the nucleous was proved by the work of Hammerling on single celled alga Acetabularia.

questions.text: Resolution power is the ability to?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Enhance color contrast in an image
options.explanation: Resolution power is not related to color contrast enhancement in an image. Resolution power is specifically concerned with the ability to distinguish fine details or separate two close points or objects in an image, regardless of the color or contrast.

Distinguish between two close points is the correct description of resolution power. It is the capacity of an imaging system to differentiate or resolve fine details that are close together. In other words, it determines the level of detail that can be observed and distinguished in an image.

Distinguish amongst organelles is not an accurate description of resolution power. While resolution power is relevant in microscopy to distinguish fine details, such as organelles within a cell, it is not specific to distinguishing amongst organelles.

Magnify an image is not the same as resolution power. Magnification refers to the increase in apparent size of an object, whereas resolution power relates to the ability to distinguish fine details or resolve separate points or objects.

questions.explanation: Resolution power is the ability to distinguish between two close objects. It is the ability of a system to distinguish between two close points as two separate points. Resolving power of a microscope depends upon the numerical aperature of the objective lens system and its wavelength.

questions.text: Acetabularia used in Hammerling’s nucleocytoplasmic experiments is a/an:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Fungi are a separate kingdom in the domain Eukarya and are known for being multicellular organisms. They consist of hyphae, which form a mycelium and reproduce through spores. Acetabularia is not a fungus, and it is not unicellular.
As mentioned earlier, fungi are typically multicellular organisms. Acetabularia is not a fungus, so this option is incorrect.

This option is correct. Acetabularia is a unicellular green alga belonging to the family Polyphysaceae. It is well-known for its large size and distinct morphological features. Acetabularia has a single cell that is uninucleate, meaning it contains a single nucleus. It is an excellent model organism for nucleocytoplasmic studies and was extensively used in Hammerling's experiments to investigate the role of the nucleus in determining cell development and characteristics.
Acetabularia is unicellular, but it has a single nucleus. It is not multinucleate, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Acetabularia is a unicellular green alga belonging to the family Polyphysaceae. It is well-known for its large size and distinct morphological features. Acetabularia has a single cell that is uninucleate, meaning it contains a single nucleus. It is an excellent model organism for nucleocytoplasmic studies and was extensively used in Hammerling's experiments to investigate the role of the nucleus in determining cell development and characteristics.

questions.text: Plastids are only found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Plastids are not found in animals. They are only found in plants.
Plastids are not found in animals. They are only found in plants.
Plastids are not found in animals. They are only found in plants.
Plastids are not found in animals. They are only found in plants.

questions.explanation: Plastids are present in autotrophic eukaryotes and multicellular organisms such as plants and algae. Animals are heterotrophs and viruses are not considered as living organisms.

questions.text: The passive, rapid exchange of materials through carrier proteins across the plasma membrane is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Passive diffusion does not occur through the carrier proteins. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Active transport is an active process as it requires energy it is not passive. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Endocytosis is the process by which cells engulf molecules, it is an active process not passive. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Facilitated diffusion also known as facilitated transport or passive-mediated transport, is the process of spontaneous passive transport of molecules or ions across a biological membrane via specific trans-membrane integral proteins.

questions.explanation: Facilitated diffusion also known as facilitated transport or passive-mediated transport, is the process of spontaneous passive transport of molecules or ions across a biological membrane via specific trans-membrane integral proteins.

questions.text: Which of the following structure is involved in oxidative phosphorylation?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: ETC occurs in inner mitochondrial membrane which provides the site for pumping of protons across it. Oxidative phosphorylation is associated with ETC.
ETC occurs in inner mitochondrial membrane which provides the site for pumping of protons across it. Oxidative phosphorylation is associated with ETC.
ETC occurs in inner mitochondrial membrane which provides the site for pumping of protons across it. Oxidative phosphorylation is associated with ETC.
ETC occurs in inner mitochondrial membrane which provides the site for pumping of protons across it. Oxidative phosphorylation is associated with ETC.

questions.explanation: ETC occurs in inner mitochondrial membrane which provides the site for pumping of protons across it. Oxidative phosphorylation is associated with ETC.

questions.text: The distinguishing feature of prokaryotic cell is its:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The chemical nature of DNA is not the distinguishing feature of prokaryotic cells. Both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells contain DNA, which carries genetic information. However, prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus where DNA is enclosed within a membrane, unlike eukaryotic cells.
Their cell wall is made of peptidoglycan.

Ribosomes are involved in protein synthesis and are found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. While there are some differences in the size and structure of ribosomes between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, the presence of ribosomes is not the primary distinguishing feature of prokaryotic cells.

Cytoplasmic streaming movement is not a characteristic that specifically distinguishes prokaryotic cells. This movement refers to the flow of cytoplasm within a cell, which can occur in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.

questions.explanation: The most distinguished feature of a prokaryotic cell is its cell wall and chemically, it is composed of carbohydrates and short peptides. This complex molecule is called peptidoglycan/ murein.

questions.text: In prokaryotic cell wall, strengthening material is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is present in plant cell wall.
It is not present in prokaryotic cell wall.
It is present in fungal cell wall.
It is present in prokaryotic cell wall.

questions.explanation: Bacterial cell wall is composed of peptidoglycan also called murein, which is made from polysaccharide chains cross-linked by short peptides. Bacterial cell walls are different from the cell walls of plants and fungi which are made of cellulose and chitin, respectively.

questions.text: Centriole is made up of _ microtubules.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Most centrioles are made up of triplet of nine microtubules. Hence centrioles have (9*3) 27 microtubules.
Most centrioles are made up of triplet of nine microtubules. Hence centrioles have (9*3) 27 microtubules.
Most centrioles are made up of triplet of nine microtubules. Hence centrioles have (9*3) 27 microtubules.
Most centrioles are made up of triplet of nine microtubules. Hence centrioles have (9*3) 27 microtubules.

questions.explanation: Most centrioles are made up of triplet of nine microtubules. Hence centrioles have (9*3) 27 microtubules.

questions.text: Out of the given options, choose the one which shows the structures found only in plants?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes and vacuoles are not found only in plant cells.
Peroxisomes and microtubules are found in all eukaryotic cells.

options.text: Chloroplast, Cell Wall, Large central vacuole
options.explanation: Chloroplast, cell wall and a large central vacuole are present only in plants.
MItochondria are found in all eukaryotic cells.

questions.explanation: Chloroplast, cell wall and large central vacuole are the organelles which are absent in animal cells but present in plant cells.

questions.text: Elaioplasts found in plants are actually one of the types of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Elaioplasts found in plants are actually one of the types of plastids but more specifically leucoplasts..
Chromoplasts are plastids that are associtaed with bright colours.
Elaioplasts found in plants are actually one of the types of leucoplasts.
Elaioplasts found in plants are actually one of the types of leucoplasts.

questions.explanation: Elaioplasts found in plants are actually one of the types of leucoplasts.

questions.text: Protein layers which are NOT continuous on the surface make _ percent of total in plasma membrane.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: According to this option, protein layers that are not continuous on the surface make up 50-60 per cent of the total proteins in the plasma membrane. This range implies that these non-continuous protein layers constitute approximately half to slightly more than half of the total protein content in the plasma membrane. Which is 60-80.

According to this option, protein layers that are not continuous on the surface make up 60-70 per cent of the total proteins in the plasma membrane. This suggests that these non-continuous protein layers represent a significant majority, ranging from around three-fifths to nearly two-thirds, of the total protein content in the plasma membrane.

The plasma membrane is composed of lipids, proteins, and carbohydrates. Proteins play an important role in the structure and function of the membrane, including the transport of molecules, cell recognition, and communication between cells. The protein layers on the surface of the plasma membrane are not continuous, and they make up approximately 60-80% of the total proteins in the membrane. Option C, 60-80%, is the correct answer.

According to this option, protein layers that are not continuous on the surface make up 70-80 per cent of the total proteins in the plasma membrane. This suggests that these non-continuous protein layers represent a significant majority, ranging from around seven-tenths to four-fifths, of the total protein content in the plasma membrane.

questions.explanation: The plasma membrane is composed of lipids, proteins, and carbohydrates. Proteins play an important role in the structure and function of the membrane, including transport of molecules, cell recognition, and communication between cells. The protein layers on the surface of the plasma membrane are not continuous, and they make up approximately 60-80% of the total proteins in the membrane. Option C, 60-80%, is the correct answer.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly shows structures which are found in a eukaryotic cell? (Yes = present ; No = absent)
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes, mitochondria and nuclear membrane are present.
Ribosomes and nuclear membrane are present.
Ribosomes and mitochondria are present.
Mitochondria are present.
Eukaryotic cells contain a nuclear membrane, mitochondria, and ribosomes. So option E is the only correct answer

questions.explanation: The question asks for eukaryotic cells, which possess a nucleus as well as other membrane bound organelles such as mitochondria, lysosomes, golgi apparatus, endoplasmic reticulum in addition to (80S) ribosomes. Only option E fulfills this criteria, as all other options have one or more of the components missing.

questions.text: Centrifugation is a _
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Process in which samples are spun at high speed
options.explanation: Centrifugation is a method of separating molecules having different densities by spinning them in solution around an axis at high speed.
Biofabrication is usually defined as the production of complex biologic products from raw materials such as living cells, matrices, biomaterials, and molecules.
Centrifugation is not a slow process.

questions.explanation: Centrifugation is a method of separating molecules having different densities by spinning them in solution around an axis at high speed. Hence option A is correct.

questions.text: If 15 um size object is observed under light microscope, using 5X eyepiece and 10X objective, its magnified image size will be:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Recall the formula for magnification, where Image Size (I)= Actual Size (A) x Magnification (M), or I=A x M. The magnification is the product of the eyepiece lens and objective lens’ respective magnifications so; M= 5 x 10 = 50. The question asks for the size of the image so; I=A x M = 15 x 50 = 750 um.
This option is incorrect.
This option is incorrect.

Recall the formula for magnification, where Image Size (I)= Actual Size (A) x Magnification (M), or I=A x M. The magnification is the product of the eyepiece lens and objective lens’ respective magnifications so; M= 5 x 10 = 50. The question asks for the size of the image so; I=A x M = 15 x 50 = 750 um.

questions.explanation: Recall the formula for magnification, where Image Size (I)= Actual Size (A) x Magnification (M), or I=A x M. The magnification is the product of the eyepiece lens and objective lens’ respective magnifications so; M= 5 x 10 = 50. The question asks for the size of the image so; I=A x M = 15 x 50 = 750 um.

questions.text: The cell organelles involved, in some cases, in extra-cellular digestion are _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Golgi complex is an organelle that is located inside a cell. It is just involved in packing the material and does not digest anything. Hence, it cannot perform extracellular digestion, and the option is incorrect.

Lysosomes are the only organelles in these options that contain digestive enzymes. They can also release their enzymes outside the cell, leading to extracellular digestion. Hence, this option is the correct one.
Peroxisomes are not involved in digestion directly, so this option is also incorrect.
Glyoxysomes are also not involved in digestion, nor do they ever leave the cell. Hence, they cannot be involved in extracellular digestion, and the option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Lysosomes function as the digestive system of the cell, serving both to degrade material taken up from outside the cell and to digest obsolete components of the cell itself.

questions.text: Animal cells are interconnected by which of the following?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The plasma membrane is the outer layer of all cells. It is a phospholipid bilayer that protects the cell and controls the movement of molecules into and out of the cell.
The cell wall is a rigid layer that surrounds some cells, such as plant cells. It provides support and protection for the cell. Animal cells do not have cell walls.

Animal cells are interconnected by desmosomes. Desmosomes are specialized junctions that help to connect the cell membranes of adjacent cells. They are made up of protein plaques that are linked by filaments. Desmosomes help to give cells strength and flexibility. They also play a role in cell signaling.
Plasmodesmata are only found in plant cells. They are channels that connect the cytoplasm of adjacent cells. Plasmodesmata allow for the movement of molecules and ions between cells.

questions.explanation: Correct option is C. Animal cells are interconnected by desmosomes. A desmosome or macula adherens can be defined as a specialized cellular structure which helps in cell-to-cell adhesion. It is formed from protein plaques which are present in the cell membranes and are linked by filaments. They are found in animal cells which act like welds between adjacent epithelial cells and connect them. So, the correct answer is Desmosomes.

questions.text: In the plasma membrane, lipid helps in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cholesterol is a lipid that alters the flexibility of the cell membrane.
Since many non-polar substances are chemicals, the lipid bilayer also helps maintain a specific chemical climate.
In the plasma membrane, the lipids help in the transport of molecules, because the lipid-soluble non-polar substances can easily diffuse through them.

In the plasma membrane, the lipids help in the transport of molecules, because the lipid-soluble non-polar substances can easily diffuse through them. Since many non-polar substances are chemicals, the lipid bilayer also helps maintain a specific chemical climate. Additionally, cholesterol is a lipid that alters the flexibility of the cell membrane. Hence, "All of these" would be the best option.

questions.explanation: In the plasma membrane, the lipids help in the transport of molecules, because the lipid-soluble non-polar substances can easily diffuse through them. Since many non-polar substances are chemicals, the lipid bilayer also helps maintain a specific chemical climate. Additionally, cholesterol is a lipid that alters the flexibility of the cell membrane. Hence, "All of these" would be the best option.

questions.text: Myofilaments are made up of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Myofilaments do not contain minerals.
Myofilaments do not contain lipids.
Myofilaments contain proteins.
Myofilaments do not contain carbohydrates.

questions.explanation: Myofilaments are composed of thick and thin filaments called myosin and actin respectively. These are proteins in nature and provide contractility to the muscle.

questions.text: Which of the following cytoskeletal fibres contain tubulin protein?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: One which helps in assembly of spindles during mitosis
options.explanation: The cytoskeletal fibre that contains tubulin protein is the one that helps in the assembling of spindle fibres during mitosis. One very important function of microtubules is their role in the assembling and disassembling of the spindle structure during mitosis.

options.text: One involved in internal cell motion
options.explanation: The actin cytoskeleton is thought to provide protrusive and contractile forces and microtubules to form a polarized network allowing the organelle and protein movement throughout cell.

options.text: One involved in maintenance of cell shape
options.explanation: There are three main types of cytoskeletal polymers; actin filaments, microtubules, and a group of polymers known collectively as intermediate filaments. Together these polymers control the shape and mechanics of eukaryotic cells.
This is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: The cytoskeletal fibre that contains tubulin protein is the one that helps in the assembling of spindle fibres during mitosis. One very important function of microtubules is their role in the assembling and disassembling of the spindle structure during mitosis.
